title: Request Lines, Methods, Target Forms, Versions, and Routing Inputs · Language Lens: Request Line Delimiters
summary: A parser uses Fields on the request line, normalizes targets before validation, treats every target as origin-form, and accepts invalid CONNECT authorities and absolute-form credentials. Planned artifact: Add and verify the request line delimiters increment in strict request-line and request-target parser. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.
Separate byte recognition, structural validation, semantic validation, policy, and effects so failure cannot partially authorize work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Request Line Delimiters · Theory Request Line Delimiters
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 7533 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to select the request-target form without normalizing away the raw routing boundary; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then split every structural delimiter across a different read and retain the same result. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
